title: psychotria merrillii var. polyneura
intro: Psychotria merrillii var. polyneura, also known as the wild coffee plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of the Philippines and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate pain, as well as promote wound healing and improve overall health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used to make a tea or infused in oil to be used topically, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, headache, and digestive issues.
